name: cryptography

metadata.name: cryptography
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: cryptography is a package which provides cryptographic recipes and primitives to Python developers.

documentation:
pyca/cryptography
=================

.. image:: https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cryptography.svg
    :target: https://pypi.org/project/cryptography/
    :alt: Latest Version

.. image:: https://readthedocs.org/projects/cryptography/badge/?version=latest
    :target: https://cryptography.io
    :alt: Latest Docs

.. image:: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/workflows/CI/badge.svg?branch=main
    :target: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/actions?query=workflow%3ACI+branch%3Amain


``cryptography`` is a package which provides cryptographic recipes and
primitives to Python developers. Our goal is for it to be your "cryptographic
standard library". It supports Python 3.7+ and PyPy3 7.3.11+.

``cryptography`` includes both high level recipes and low level interfaces to
common cryptographic algorithms such as symmetric ciphers, message digests, and
key derivation functions. For example, to encrypt something with
``cryptography``'s high level symmetric encryption recipe:

.. code-block:: pycon

    >>> from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
    >>> # Put this somewhere safe!
    >>> key = Fernet.generate_key()
    >>> f = Fernet(key)
    >>> token = f.encrypt(b"A really secret message. Not for prying eyes.")
    >>> token
    b'...'
    >>> f.decrypt(token)
    b'A really secret message. Not for prying eyes.'

You can find more information in the `documentation`_.

You can install ``cryptography`` with:

.. code-block:: console

    $ pip install cryptography

For full details see `the installation documentation`_.

Discussion
~~~~~~~~~~

If you run into bugs, you can file them in our `issue tracker`_.

We maintain a `cryptography-dev`_ mailing list for development discussion.

You can also join ``#pyca`` on ``irc.libera.chat`` to ask questions or get
involved.

Security
~~~~~~~~

Need to report a security issue? Please consult our `security reporting`_
documentation.


.. _`documentation`: https://cryptography.io/
.. _`the installation documentation`: https://cryptography.io/en/latest/installation/
.. _`issue tracker`: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/issues
.. _`cryptography-dev`: https://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/cryptography-dev
.. _`security reporting`: https://cryptography.io/en/latest/security/

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.CipherContext.update

Processes the provided bytes through the cipher and returns the results

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.CipherContext.update_into

Processes the provided bytes and writes the resulting data into the

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.CipherContext.finalize
documentation: Returns the results of processing the final block as bytes.

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.CipherContext.reset_nonce
documentation:
Resets the nonce for the cipher context to the provided value.
Raises an exception if it does not support reset or if the
provided nonce does not have a valid length.

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.AEADCipherContext.authenticate_additional_data
documentation: Authenticates the provided bytes.

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.AEADDecryptionContext.finalize_with_tag
documentation:
Returns the results of processing the final block as bytes and allows

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.base.AEADEncryptionContext.tag
documentation:
Returns tag bytes. This is only available after encryption is
finalized.

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa._modinv
documentation: Modular Multiplicative Inverse. Returns x such that: (x*e) mod m == 1

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa.rsa_crt_iqmp
documentation: Compute the CRT (q ** -1) % p value from RSA primes p and q.

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa.rsa_crt_dmp1
documentation:
Compute the CRT private_exponent % (p - 1) value from the RSA
private_exponent (d) and p.

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa.rsa_crt_dmq1
documentation:
Compute the CRT private_exponent % (q - 1) value from the RSA
private_exponent (d) and q.

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa.rsa_recover_private_exponent
documentation:
Compute the RSA private_exponent (d) given the public exponent (e)
and the RSA primes p and q.

This uses the Carmichael totient function to generate the
smallest possible working value of the private exponent.

functions.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa.rsa_recover_prime_factors
documentation:
Compute factors p and q from the private exponent d. We assume that n has
no more than two factors. This function is adapted from code in PyCrypto.

classes.name: cryptography.x509.certificate_transparency.SignatureAlgorithm
documentation:
Signature algorithms that are valid for SCTs.

These are exactly the same as SignatureAlgorithm in RFC 5246 (TLS 1.2).

See: <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5246#section-7.4.1.4.1>

functions.name: _cffi_src.utils.build_ffi_for_binding
documentation:
Modules listed in `modules` should have the following attributes:

- `INCLUDES`: A string containing C includes.
- `TYPES`: A string containing C declarations for types.
- `FUNCTIONS`: A string containing C declarations for functions & macros.
- `CUSTOMIZATIONS`: A string containing arbitrary top-level C code, this
  : can be used to do things like test for a define and provide an
    alternate implementation based on that.

documentation: This file is dual licensed under the terms of the Apache License, Version
2.0, and the BSD License. See the LICENSE file in the root of this repository
for complete details.

functions.name: cryptography.x509.name._escape_dn_value
documentation: Escape special characters in RFC4514 Distinguished Name value.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.name.NameAttribute.rfc4514_attribute_name
documentation:
The short attribute name (for example "CN") if available,
otherwise the OID dotted string.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.name.NameAttribute.rfc4514_string
documentation:
Format as RFC4514 Distinguished Name string.

Use short attribute name if available, otherwise fall back to OID
dotted string.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.name.RelativeDistinguishedName.rfc4514_string
documentation:
Format as RFC4514 Distinguished Name string.

Within each RDN, attributes are joined by '+', although that is rarely
used in certificates.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.name.Name.rfc4514_string
documentation:
Format as RFC4514 Distinguished Name string.
For example 'CN=foobar.com,O=Foo Corp,C=US'

An X.509 name is a two-level structure: a list of sets of attributes.
Each list element is separated by ',' and within each list element, set
elements are separated by '+'. The latter is almost never used in
real world certificates. According to RFC4514 section 2.1 the
RDNSequence must be reversed when converting to string representation.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.name._RFC4514NameParser.parse
documentation:
Parses the `data` string and converts it to a Name.

According to RFC4514 section 2.1 the RDNSequence must be
reversed when converting to string representation. So, when
we parse it, we need to reverse again to get the RDNs on the
correct order.

classes.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.ssh._SSHFormatSKEd25519

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.ssh._SSHFormatSKEd25519.load_public
documentation: Make Ed25519 public key from data.

The format of a sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com public key is:

    string          "sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com"
    string          public key
    string          application (user-specified, but typically "ssh:")

classes.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.ssh._SSHFormatSKECDSA

methods.name: cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.ssh._SSHFormatSKECDSA.load_public
documentation: Make ECDSA public key from data.

The format of a sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com public key is:

    string          "sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com"
    string          curve name
    ec_point        Q
    string          application (user-specified, but typically "ssh:")

functions.name: cryptography.x509.base._convert_to_naive_utc_time
documentation:
Normalizes a datetime to a naive datetime in UTC.

time -- datetime to normalize. Assumed to be in UTC if not timezone
        aware.

documentation: Returns the ObjectIdentifier of the public key.

documentation: Not after time (represented as UTC datetime)

in the certificate.

documentation:
Returns the tbsCertificate payload bytes with the SCT list extension
stripped.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.Certificate.__eq__
documentation: Checks equality.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.Certificate.__hash__
documentation: Computes a hash.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.Certificate.public_bytes
documentation: Serializes the certificate to PEM or DER format.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.Certificate.verify_directly_issued_by
documentation:
This method verifies that certificate issuer name matches the
issuer subject name and that the certificate is signed by the
issuer's private key. No other validation is performed.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.RevokedCertificate.serial_number
documentation: Returns the serial number of the revoked certificate.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.RevokedCertificate.revocation_date
documentation: Returns the date of when this certificate was revoked.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.RevokedCertificate.revocation_date_utc
documentation:
Returns the date of when this certificate was revoked as a non-naive
UTC datetime.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.RevokedCertificate.extensions
documentation: Returns an Extensions object containing a list of Revoked extensions.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.public_bytes
documentation: Serializes the CRL to PEM or DER format.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.fingerprint
documentation: Returns bytes using digest passed.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.get_revoked_certificate_by_serial_number
documentation:
Returns an instance of RevokedCertificate or None if the serial_number
is not in the CRL.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.signature_hash_algorithm
documentation:
Returns a HashAlgorithm corresponding to the type of the digest signed
in the certificate.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.signature_algorithm_oid
documentation: Returns the ObjectIdentifier of the signature algorithm.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.signature_algorithm_parameters
documentation: Returns the signature algorithm parameters.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.issuer
documentation: Returns the X509Name with the issuer of this CRL.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.next_update
documentation: Returns the date of next update for this CRL.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.next_update_utc
documentation:
Returns the date of next update for this CRL as a non-naive UTC
datetime.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.last_update
documentation: Returns the date of last update for this CRL.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.last_update_utc
documentation:
Returns the date of last update for this CRL as a non-naive UTC
datetime.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.extensions
documentation: Returns an Extensions object containing a list of CRL extensions.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.signature
documentation: Returns the signature bytes.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.tbs_certlist_bytes

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.__eq__
documentation: Checks equality.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.__len__

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.__getitem__

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.__iter__
documentation: Iterator over the revoked certificates

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateRevocationList.is_signature_valid
documentation: Verifies signature of revocation list against given public key.

documentation: Returns the signature algorithm parameters.

documentation: Sets the certificate requestor's distinguished name.

documentation: Adds an X.509 extension to the certificate request.

documentation: Adds an X.509 attribute with an OID and associated value.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateSigningRequestBuilder.sign
documentation: Signs the request using the requestor's private key.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateBuilder.issuer_name
documentation: Sets the CA's distinguished name.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateBuilder.subject_name
documentation: Sets the requestor's distinguished name.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateBuilder.serial_number

documentation: Adds an X.509 extension to the certificate.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.base.CertificateBuilder.sign
documentation: Signs the certificate using the CA's private key.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.extensions.ExtensionType.public_bytes
documentation: Serializes the extension type to DER.

methods.name: cryptography.x509.extensions.AuthorityKeyIdentifier.from_issuer_public_key
documentation: This takes a subset of CertificatePublicKeyTypes because an issuer
cannot have an X25519/X448 key. This introduces some unfortunate
asymmetry that requires typing users to explicitly
narrow their type, but we should make this accurate and not just
convenient.
